author: potuz
content: I feel rather safe given that it takes many orders of magnitude more to review code than to write it now.

author: funnygiulio
content: anyways I think vibe coded clients are not a good allocation of time. building tooling is 100x better imo. if the objective is "replacing core devs", I would spend my time writing tooling for existing software as you will have to write that same tooling for vibecoded software anyways

author: sky.cactus
content: I'm working on this problem now.  I realized the same thing and that the number one bottleneck is review, not authoring.  Figuring out how to orchestrate agents to automate review in a way that doesn't miss problems, and can autonomously identify and deal with false positives.  I'm optimistic this is a solvable problem with sufficient engineering.

author: sky.cactus
content: I hope you like herding goats!  That is my backup plan.
